title: Job Openings at Ignite Energy Access
content:
Ignite Energy Access is an Abu Dhabi-based leader in distributed renewable energy solutions, dedicated to providing affordable, reliable, and sustainable electricity to underserved communities across Africa. Now operating across 12 countries and serving over 15million people. Through innovative technology, scalable business models, and impact-driven financing, Ignite Power is transforming lives, fostering economic growth, and driving the continent’s clean energy transition.

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.) Team Lead

Job Locations: Akwa Ibom , Anambra, Bayelsa, Delta, Imo, and Rivers

Job Purpose / Mission
     

            
    




	Regional Operations: This position will be part of the Mysol Sales team across any region of operation in Nigeria, supporting and enabling frontline sales agents to sell more efficiently and responsibly.
	Employment Status: The Sales Team Lead role is not afull-timerole.
	Compensation: Position includes a base pay along with performance-based commission incentives, in line with the applicable incentive scheme notified by Mysol from time to time.
	Flexibility: Reporting lines and duty stations may change over time to reflect the Scope of Duties and the needs of the company.
	Core Expertise: The Sales Team Lead must possess in-depth knowledge of sales, agent  recruitment and retention, team management, and proven experience leading a team.


Key Responsibilities


	Agent Management & Recruitment: Recruit, train, and oversee a minimum of 10 active sales agents monthly, ensuring continuous development and achievement of performance targets.
	Sales Pipeline Management: Manage the sales process of Mysol products to potential and new customers in the designated territory.
	Financial Compliance: Supervise sales transactions and ensure timely and accurate remittance of proceeds according to company policies.
	Credit Control: For credit-based sales, take full ownership of credit follow-ups and collection.
	Asset Management: Oversee and manage the assigned company's tools of trade, ensuring their proper care and maintenance.
	Budget Optimization: Utilize allocated funds judiciously to support sales activities, maximizing their impact on revenue generation, sales volume improvement, and market expansion.
	Strategic Planning: Formulate and execute operational sales strategies within the assigned area, aligning with area sales targets set by the Regional Business Manager.
	Field Leadership: Coordinate and lead field sales activities to meet sales targets and maintain high-quality sales standards.
	Training & Coaching: Provide comprehensive training and coaching to sales agents covering key areas such as pitching, sales conversations, closing techniques, and fostering enduring customer relationships.
	Performance Reviews: Conduct regular performance evaluations for sales agents, offering constructive feedback and guidance for improvement.
	Inventory Control: Support monthly inventory audits for both the service center and Point of Sale (POS) locations in the assigned area.


Qualifications


	National Diploma (ND), Higher National Diploma (HND), or Bachelor of Science (BSc) in Business Administration, Marketing, or a related field.


Experience:


	1 - 2 years of related sales experience. Relevant experience in selling solar products is highly preferred.


Knowledge and skills:


	Proven leadership capabilities and the ability to successfully deliver exceptional service in relation to the terms and conditions of the contract
	Leadership:Strong leadership and team management abilities.
	Communication:Good communication skills with the ability to state messages clearly, using language that is easily comprehensible to others.
	Core Competencies:Strong problem-solving skills, planning capabilities, highly organized, and a dedicated team player.
	Stakeholder Management:Ability to manage relationships with different stakeholders to resolve issues of moderate complexity.
	Adaptability:Ability to react quickly and effectively to changing priorities.


Language(s):


	English.


Application Closing Date: 28th August, 2026.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position and preferred location as the subject of the mail. 

 

2.) Regional Business Manager

Job Locations: Akwa Ibom, Anambra, Bayelsa, Delta, Imo, Katsina, Sokoto & Zamfara
Employment Type: Full-time (100% Field Based)

 

Job Purpose / Mission


	The Regional Business Manager is responsible for driving Ignite Energy Accesscommercial performance across the assigned region by leading high-performing sales teams, managing regional assets, and translating national strategy into effective regional execution.
	Owns the full business performance of the assigned region, including revenue generation, profitability, cost management, and credit performance, ensuring that commercial targets are consistently met through disciplined operational oversight.
	The role designs and implements scalable sales and customer credit strategies, leveraging data on sales patterns, customer demographics, and repayment behaviour to develop targeted, data-driven initiatives suited to a large and underserved consumer base in emerging markets.
	The role leads the recruitment, training, and retention of a motivated field sales team including Team Leads and Mysol Power Championswhile managing all regional assets such as Service Centres, stock, personnel, and marketing collaterals to deliver business goals efficiently.
	It contributes significantly to IEA's strategic planning process at the regional level, with insightsfrom the region informing expansion into new markets as the organisation scales across Nigeria.
	The role is field-based within the assigned region and requires frequent intra- and interstate travel to ensure close oversight of operations and team performance.


Key Responsibilities
Commercial Leadership & Strategy:


	Lead the development and execution of Regional Business Strategies aligned with national goals
	Deliver on Sales volume, revenue and credit portfolio targets for the region
	Identify and pursue opportunities for regional market expansion and penetration
	Generate market intelligence and insights to inform national commercial planning
	Acquire new customers and increase repeat sales for our most creditworthy existing customers
	Own and advance relationships with key stakeholders
	Provide leadership to the teams that drive sales and customer repayment
	Train and continuously re-train field sales teams to improve capability and drive efficiency.


Credit & Portfolio Performance:


	Continuously improve customer repayment to drive healthy portfolio
	Implement strategies to enhance customer lifetime value through sustainable repayment and repeat sales
	Ensure strong collaboration with credit operations and support teams to minimize portfolio risk


Market Development & Stakeholder Engagement:


	Create and own relationship management of key stakeholders within the sales process, especially with distribution partners (if any), Critical support functions, etc
	Support in creating and maintaining relationships with current and potential commercial partners
	Represent the company with agencies and communities as needed


People and Team leadership:


	Recruit, develop and retain top performing sales and field teams across the region
	Provide leadership, coaching and performance management for sales teams
	Drive a culture of accountability, collaboration and customer centricity within sales teams


Sales and Marketing initiatives:


	Test, validate, and refine IEA Nigeria’s strategic sales and marketing initiatives based on analysis of our unique data set and field-based insights, in order to amplify our sales model to drive new customer acquisition


Operational & Resource Management:


	Ensure optimal use of regional assets and marketing collateral
	Monitor cost efficiency and ensure profitable business operations within the region
	Uphold compliance with company policies, governance frameworks and regulatory requirements
	Oversee inventory management within the region ensuring accurate stock records, timely replenishment and efficient distribution to sales channels
	Monitor stock and implement controls to safeguard company assets


Data Based Insights:


	Use our unique data set as well as insights derived from the field to refine and iterate on our customer retention strategy, in order to improve payment velocity, repayment rates and repeat sales.


Change & Transformation:


	Act as a change leader in implementing new organizational structures, processes and digital tools
	Support national transformation programs by ensuring adoption and alignment within the region
	Continuously refine regional commercial strategies based on field learnings and evolving market realities


Knowledge and skills:


	Proven experience and a passion for motivating and leading large teams to deliver ambitious goals within a commercial business environment.
	Ability to draw the right insights from data to create and nimbly adjust strategy, make difficult decisions that often require tradeoffs, balanced with a bias to action.
	Experience or passion for working in developing markets Highly Desired Skills
	Ability to  work both in teams and independently.
	Passionate about solving problems for customers in rural communities.
	Proficiency in English Language and 1 or more other Nigerian Languages
	Critical thinking skills (ability to think outside the box)
	Good problem-solving skills (solutions oriented)
	Flexibility with ability to work under pressure to meet deadlines.
	Proficiency in Word, Excel, and PowerPoint
	Great Analytical skills
	Excellent written & verbal communication skills.


Qualifications


	HND / Bachelor’s Degree in any discipline.


Experience:


	5+ years of work experience working in Mid/senior management levels within a progressive renewable energy, sale and/or financial services environment with strong, quantifiable results.


Language(s):


	English
	Other indigenous (nice to have).


Application Closing Date: 3rd August, 2026.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the  Job Position and preferred Location as the subject of the mail.

 

3.) Zonal Team Lead  

Job Locations: Kogi and Nasarawa
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Purpose / Mission


	Ignite Energy Access is looking for a dynamic, highly motivated and disciplined Customer Finance Zonal Team Lead with a passion for Recovery, Stakeholder’s management, entrepreneurship, renewable energy and financial inclusion.
	This position is a key role within the Customer Finance Department of a fast-paced start up on the front lines of renewable energy and mobile payment technology.
	This role requires individuals to work under limited supervision, perform duties associated with locating unreachable customers, direct interaction with customers, and where necessary, deactivating non-paying customers while giving good customer experience.


Key Responsibilities
Target Fulfillment and Performance Management:


	Reach a minimum of all quantitative and qualitative targets set by the customer finance department on Bounce backs, Reactivations and Repossession.
	Keep the Write-off Value within an acceptable threshold as defined by the Customer


Finance Department:


	Develop a scalable credit portfolio maintenance and recovery process for the Region.
	Monthly performance review and training for performance improvement with the Customer finance representative team within the region.
	Manage repayment Payment integration within the respective regions.


Recruitment and Training: 

Recruitment& Staffing


	Recruit and maintain Collection agents that will drive the achievement of the set target.
	Conduct Credit Training activities for Each region on recovery activities.
	Recruit and maintain Collection agents that will drive the achievement of the set target
	Analyze credit data on sales results and develop operational plans and to address performance gaps and improvement.


Credit Compliance and Credit Culture:


	Facilitate Usage and implementation of Credit Management Tools within the Region.
	Carry out Credit assessment check and make informed credit decision for High End Kits.
	Conduct Credit compliance during field visits to all regions.
	Carry out on the spot assessment of Credit compliance of the field team.
	Analyze credit data on sales results and develop operational plans and to address performance gaps and improvement.


Stakeholders Management:


	Reach all quantitative and qualitative targets set by the customer finance department on Late to first payment, bounce backs, Repossession and Payment Agents.
	Carry out a quarterly credit Campaign that keeps all Credit metrics within acceptable threshold.
	Conduct Weekly recovery Committees with all Stakeholders within the Region.
	Ensure Team’s participation and every Credit related activity (Field Clinic, Credit Campaign) within the region of coverage.
	In collaboration with the Business Stakeholders department, will work with our customers to understand their needs and provide improvement to seamless and better payment channel in the zone/region.
	Manage additional ad hoc tasks as assigned by Customer Finance Department.


Qualification


	HND / B.Sc Degree or Diploma in a relevant field.


Experience:


	2- 4 years working in similar renewable energy or related field experience is a plus.
	2 - 3 years of experience as a Loan recovery Manager is an added advantage
	Experience in leading a team, and ability to work with and manage a dispersed team in rural communities.


Knowledge and skills:


	Highly Skilled in customer relations, negotiations and problem solving.
	Ability to build relationships with colleagues, cross-functional counterparts, external business partners and other middle managers
	Stong Analytical skills.
	Pay attention to details.
	A good proficiency of Data Analytics Tools (Tableau, SQL, Microsoft Excel).


Language(s):


	English.


Application Closing Date: 23rd July, 2026.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position and preferred Location as the subject of the mail. 

     

    

        Click here to Apply

Human Resource Officer Job at Artee Group




        
    

        
        Artee Group - The story of SPAR can't be told without mentioning Artee Group. Over the past 30 years, Artee Industries Limited has grown from very humble beginnings to become one of the fastest growing business conglomerates in Nigeria. The story started in 1988, with the opening of a small supermarket store measuring 120 m2 in Lagos under the Park ’n’ Shop banner. Today, its business spreads from retail to real estate, shopping malls and manufacturing, rightly gaining popularity as Artee Group.

We are  recruiting to fill the position below:

 Job Position: Human Resource Officer

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

            
    




	The HR Officer will oversee all human resources functions across VHIL locations within Lagos.
	This role involves recruitment, onboarding, policy enforcement, performance management, and HR analytics, coordinating training programs, managing staff attendance, welfare, handling queries, and overseeing general HR-related matters.


Responsibilities
Recruitment and Onboarding: 


	Collaborate with Business  Manager to identify staffing needs, conduct interviews, assess candidates, and manage the entire onboarding process for new hires.


Policy Enforcement: 


	Ensure compliance with company policies and procedures across all locations. Implement and communicate HR policies in line with business goals.


Performance Management: 


	Oversee performance appraisals to evaluate employee performance, providing guidance and support to managers in addressing performance-related matters.


HR Analytics and Reporting:


	Utilize HR data to generate reports, and provide insights to drive informed decision-making.
	Prepare regular HR reports for management review.


Training Program Coordination:


	Organize and coordinate in-house training programs to enhance employee skills and knowledge.


Attendance Management:


	Monitor and manage staff attendance records and leave requests across locations.


Employee Welfare and Queries:


	Act as a point of contact for employee queries, concerns, and grievances.
	Facilitate resolution of employee issues in collaboration with relevant stakeholders.


General HR Matters:


	Oversee all other general HR-related activities, ensuring adherence to legal requirements and best practices.


Key Performance Indicator


	Time-to-Fill: Decrease time-to-fill without compromising candidate quality.
	Employee Turnover Rate: Maintain a low turnover rate by implementing retention strategies.
	Policy Compliance Rate: Maintain a high rate of policy compliance across all locations.
	Performance Evaluation Completion Rate: Ensure timely completion of performance evaluations for all employees.
	Absenteeism Rate: Reduce absenteeism by implementing strategies to improve attendance.
	Employee Satisfaction and Engagement: Maintain or improve overall employee satisfaction and engagement scores throughout employee lifecycle.
	HR Metrics Reporting Accuracy: Ensure precise and timely reporting to aid decision-making.
	Quick TAT on Staff requests (e.g Confirmation, leave, loan, IOU etc).
	Training & Development- Designing Effective Training Plan and Strategies to increase employee efficiency and motivation. Not less than 80% achievement of Training Plan.
	Manpower Adequacy level-Ensuring 100% utilization of budgeted manpower.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

Operations Manager Job at Enoella Consult




        
    

        
        Enoella Consult - Our client, a leading Inspection & Integrity Services company (NDT, Drill Pipe/BHA, OCTG, Calibration, Lifting Equipment) serving the oil & gas, marine, and industrial sectors, is  recruiting qualified professionals to fill the position below:

 Job Position: Operations  Manager 

Job Location: Port Harcourt, Rivers
Employment Type: Full-time

Qualifications
     

            
    




	Bachelor's Degree or HND in Engineering, Applied Sciences, Industrial Technology, or related discipline
	2–5 years in oil & gas operations, including at least 3 years in a supervisory/management role





Key Requirements:


	Plan, coordinate and supervise operations across NDT, drill pipe inspection, calibration and related services
	Strong knowledge of API, ASTM, ASNT and ISO standards
	Manage manpower, equipment, logistics and project scheduling
	Ensure HSSE, radiation safety and QMS compliance
	Excellent leadership, negotiation and reporting skills
	Proficient in Microsoft Office and reporting tools.


Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Business Development Manager – Forex Brokerage",
  "description": "ACCM Global Enterprise Limited is a dynamic financial services company committed to providing innovative investment solutions and empowering individuals and businesses to achieve their financial goals. Through a client-focused approach, we offer access to financial market opportunities, investment education, and wealth-building strategies designed to support long-term financial growth.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Development Manager &ndash; Forex Brokerage

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

About the Role


	We are seeking an experienced and highly motivated Business Development Manager to drive client acquisition, revenue growth, and strategic partnerships for our Forex Brokerage business.
	The ideal candidate will have a strong understanding of the financial markets, Forex trading industry, and customer acquisition strategies, with proven experience in training and mentoring teams.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop and execute business development strategies to achieve revenue and client acquisition targets.
	Identify and onboard new retail and institutional clients.
	Build and maintain strong relationships with Introducing Brokers (IBs), affiliates, and strategic partners.
	Conduct market research to identify business opportunities and emerging industry trends.
	Present the company&#39;s products and services to prospective clients and partners.
	Drive account funding and trading activity among new and existing clients.
	Collaborate with the marketing team to generate quality leads and improve conversion rates.
	Prepare and deliver sales reports, forecasts, and market insights.
	Organize webinars, seminars, and client education sessions.
	Train and mentor business development executives and relationship managers on sales, client acquisition, and market knowledge.
	Ensure compliance with company policies and regulatory requirements.


Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)


	New client acquisition.
	Account funding volume.
	Revenue generation.
	Partnership development.
	Client conversion rate.
	Team performance and training effectiveness.


Requirements


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree in Business Administration, Finance, Economics, Marketing, or a related field.
	4 - 6 years of experience in Business Development within Forex, Financial Services, Investment, FinTech, or Brokerage industries.
	Strong knowledge of Forex trading, CFDs, financial markets, and investment products.
	Proven track record of achieving sales and revenue targets.
	Experience in team leadership, coaching, and training.
	Excellent communication, negotiation, and presentation skills.
	Proficiency in CRM systems and Microsoft Office Suite.


Salary
N700,000 - N800,000 monthly.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and a Cover Letter to: modinat@accmfx.com using &quot;Business Development Manager&quot; as the subject of the email.

Note: If you are passionate about building a career in financial markets and sales, we would love to hear from you.",

Business Development Manager – Forex Brokerage Job at ACCM Global Enterprise Limited




        
    

        
        ACCM Global Enterprise Limited is a dynamic financial services company committed to providing innovative investment solutions and empowering individuals and businesses to achieve their financial goals. Through a client-focused approach, we offer access to financial market opportunities, investment education, and wealth-building strategies designed to support long-term financial growth.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Business Development Manager – Forex Brokerage

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

About the Role
     

            
    




	We are seeking an experienced and highly motivated Business Development Manager to drive client acquisition, revenue growth, and strategic partnerships for our Forex Brokerage business.
	The ideal candidate will have a strong understanding of the financial markets, Forex trading industry, and customer acquisition strategies, with proven experience in training and mentoring teams.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop and execute business development strategies to achieve revenue and client acquisition targets.
	Identify and onboard new retail and institutional clients.
	Build and maintain strong relationships with Introducing Brokers (IBs), affiliates, and strategic partners.
	Conduct market research to identify business opportunities and emerging industry trends.
	Present the company's products and services to prospective clients and partners.
	Drive account funding and trading activity among new and existing clients.
	Collaborate with the marketing team to generate quality leads and improve conversion rates.
	Prepare and deliver sales reports, forecasts, and market insights.
	Organize webinars, seminars, and client education sessions.
	Train and mentor business development executives and relationship managers on sales, client acquisition, and market knowledge.
	Ensure compliance with company policies and regulatory requirements.


Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)


	New client acquisition.
	Account funding volume.
	Revenue generation.
	Partnership development.
	Client conversion rate.
	Team performance and training effectiveness.


Requirements


	Bachelor's Degree in Business Administration, Finance, Economics, Marketing, or a related field.
	4 - 6 years of experience in Business Development within Forex, Financial Services, Investment, FinTech, or Brokerage industries.
	Strong knowledge of Forex trading, CFDs, financial markets, and investment products.
	Proven track record of achieving sales and revenue targets.
	Experience in team leadership, coaching, and training.
	Excellent communication, negotiation, and presentation skills.
	Proficiency in CRM systems and Microsoft Office Suite.


Salary
N700,000 - N800,000 monthly.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and a Cover Letter to: [email protected] using "Business Development Manager" as the subject of the email.

Note: If you are passionate about building a career in financial markets and sales, we would love to hear from you.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Business Development Executive",
  "description": "Nondera Energy Services Limited is a dynamic oil and gas servicing company specialized in providing telecommunications, engineering, drilling, procurement and technical assistance services to clients in the Nigerian energy sector.

We maintain a sustainable competitive advantage in the industry, strategically positioning us to achieve our vision &quot;to be the leading provider of integrated energy solutions in Africa&quot;.

We are&nbsp;&nbsp;recruiting to fill the position below:

&nbsp;Job Position: Business Development Executive&nbsp;

Job Location: Lekki Phase 1, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	We are seeking a highly driven, strategic, and results-oriented Business Development Executive to join our team and support the growth of our Power Solutions and Energy Services portfolio.
	The successful candidate will be responsible for identifying, developing, and converting business opportunities across the power, renewable energy, engineering, and oil &amp; gas sectors.
	The role requires a strong understanding of energy solutions, excellent client engagement skills, and the ability to develop commercially viable solutions for industrial, commercial, and institutional customers.
	The ideal candidate will drive market expansion by building strong client relationships, generating new opportunities, supporting proposal development, and contributing to the achievement of business growth objectives.


Key Responsibilities


	Identify, develop, and secure new business opportunities within the power and energy sector, including:
	
		Solar energy solutions
		Hybrid power systems
		Captive power generation
		Mini-grid solutions
		Distributed energy systems
		Energy infrastructure projects
	
	
	Develop and maintain strong relationships with commercial, industrial, government, and institutional clients.
	Generate, qualify, and manage business leads through market research, networking, referrals, and strategic engagement.
	Conduct client meetings, understand customer requirements, and develop tailored energy solutions in collaboration with engineering teams.
	Support preparation of technical and commercial proposals, bids, tenders, presentations, and business development documents.
	&nbsp;Work closely with project, engineering, and operations teams to ensure solutions align with client expectations and business objectives.
	Conduct industry analysis, competitor assessments, and market intelligence to identify emerging opportunities.
	Support contract negotiations, partnership discussions, and commercial engagements.
	Maintain an accurate business development pipeline and provide regular reports on opportunities, progress, and forecasts.
	Represent the company at industry events, conferences, exhibitions, and professional networking platforms.


Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Engineering, Business Administration, Marketing, Economics, or a related discipline.
	3&ndash;5 years of experience in business development, technical sales, account management, or commercial roles within:
	
		Power sector
		Renewable energy industry
		Engineering services
		Oil &amp; Gas sector
	
	
	Strong understanding of renewable energy technologies, including solar PV systems, hybrid solutions, mini-grids, energy storage, and power infrastructure.
	Demonstrated ability to develop client relationships, generate leads, and achieve business growth targets.
	Excellent communication, presentation, negotiation, and stakeholder management skills.
	Experience in proposal preparation, tender processes, commercial documentation, and client acquisition.
	Ability to understand technical concepts and translate them into commercially attractive solutions.
	Self-motivated, proactive, and capable of working independently while achieving measurable results.


Preferred Experience:
Candidates with experience in the following areas will have an advantage:


	Commercial &amp; Industrial (C&amp;I) solar projects
	Hybrid renewable energy systems
	Energy-as-a-Service (EaaS) models
	Embedded power solutions
	Mini-grid development
	Power generation and infrastructure projects
	Oil &amp; Gas sector business development.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their updated CV to: careers@nondera.com using &quot;Business Development Executive -&nbsp; Power&quot; as the subject of the email.

Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.",
  "datePosted": "2026-07-13",
  "validThrough": "2026-07-25",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",
  "hiringOrganization": {
    "@type": "Organization",

Business Development Executive Job at Nondera Energy Services Limited




        
    

        
        Nondera Energy Services Limited is a dynamic oil and gas servicing company specialized in providing telecommunications, engineering, drilling, procurement and technical assistance services to clients in the Nigerian energy sector.

We maintain a sustainable competitive advantage in the industry, strategically positioning us to achieve our vision "to be the leading provider of integrated energy solutions in Africa".

We are  recruiting to fill the position below:

 Job Position: Business Development Executive 

Job Location: Lekki Phase 1, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description
     

            
    




	We are seeking a highly driven, strategic, and results-oriented Business Development Executive to join our team and support the growth of our Power Solutions and Energy Services portfolio.
	The successful candidate will be responsible for identifying, developing, and converting business opportunities across the power, renewable energy, engineering, and oil & gas sectors.
	The role requires a strong understanding of energy solutions, excellent client engagement skills, and the ability to develop commercially viable solutions for industrial, commercial, and institutional customers.
	The ideal candidate will drive market expansion by building strong client relationships, generating new opportunities, supporting proposal development, and contributing to the achievement of business growth objectives.


Key Responsibilities


	Identify, develop, and secure new business opportunities within the power and energy sector, including:
	
		Solar energy solutions
		Hybrid power systems
		Captive power generation
		Mini-grid solutions
		Distributed energy systems
		Energy infrastructure projects
	
	
	Develop and maintain strong relationships with commercial, industrial, government, and institutional clients.
	Generate, qualify, and manage business leads through market research, networking, referrals, and strategic engagement.
	Conduct client meetings, understand customer requirements, and develop tailored energy solutions in collaboration with engineering teams.
	Support preparation of technical and commercial proposals, bids, tenders, presentations, and business development documents.
	 Work closely with project, engineering, and operations teams to ensure solutions align with client expectations and business objectives.
	Conduct industry analysis, competitor assessments, and market intelligence to identify emerging opportunities.
	Support contract negotiations, partnership discussions, and commercial engagements.
	Maintain an accurate business development pipeline and provide regular reports on opportunities, progress, and forecasts.
	Represent the company at industry events, conferences, exhibitions, and professional networking platforms.


Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree in Engineering, Business Administration, Marketing, Economics, or a related discipline.
	3–5 years of experience in business development, technical sales, account management, or commercial roles within:
	
		Power sector
		Renewable energy industry
		Engineering services
		Oil & Gas sector
	
	
	Strong understanding of renewable energy technologies, including solar PV systems, hybrid solutions, mini-grids, energy storage, and power infrastructure.
	Demonstrated ability to develop client relationships, generate leads, and achieve business growth targets.
	Excellent communication, presentation, negotiation, and stakeholder management skills.
	Experience in proposal preparation, tender processes, commercial documentation, and client acquisition.
	Ability to understand technical concepts and translate them into commercially attractive solutions.
	Self-motivated, proactive, and capable of working independently while achieving measurable results.


Preferred Experience:
Candidates with experience in the following areas will have an advantage:


	Commercial & Industrial (C&I) solar projects
	Hybrid renewable energy systems
	Energy-as-a-Service (EaaS) models
	Embedded power solutions
	Mini-grid development
	Power generation and infrastructure projects
	Oil & Gas sector business development.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their updated CV to: [email protected] using "Business Development Executive -  Power" as the subject of the email.

Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.    

    

        Click here to Apply
